description_long: This is a four-story apartment building with a rectangular footprint and a flat roof. The facade is clad in reddish-brown brickwork, segmented by horizontal bands of light gray stone or concrete that mark each floor. The front features a symmetrical arrangement of twelve windows, three per floor, each with stone lintels and sills, and a central double wooden door at the ground level. The sides and rear of the building are plain, with no windows or doors, and are finished in the same brick and stone banding as the front. The structure appears robust and utilitarian, typical of early 20th-century urban residential architecture. The overall appearance suggests a focus on function and durability, with minimal decorative elements.
description: A four-story, rectangular apartment building with a brick facade, stone bands, symmetrical windows, and a central double wooden door.
description_view_0: Front view showing four floors, twelve windows in a symmetrical grid, and a central double wooden entrance door.
description_view_1: Side view displaying plain brickwork with horizontal stone bands and no windows or doors.
description_view_2: Rear view with uninterrupted brickwork and stone bands, lacking any windows or entrances.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, identical to the other side, with plain brickwork and stone bands.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a symmetrical grid on the front facade, with three evenly spaced windows per floor, aligned vertically and horizontally.
